title: pedicularis parryi subsp. mogollonica
intro: Pedicularis parryi subsp. mogollonica, also known as Mogollon Indian paintbrush, is a flowering plant species in the bumblebee-bane genus, characterized by its bright red and white flowers. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as relieving digestive issues, reducing fever, and soothing respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Pedicularis parryi subsp. mogollonica typically include infusions, tinctures, and teas, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
